--- SOURCE 5: Gene Editing - CRISPR Therapeutics ---
URL: https://crisprtx.com/gene-editing

SUMMARY:
CRISPR/Cas9 – a revolutionary gene-editing technology that can be used to modify or correct precise regions of our DNA to treat serious diseases.

FULL CONTENT:
![CRISPR](/assets/build/img/logo-main.svg)

# Gene Editing

### **CRISPR/Cas9 – a revolutionary gene-editing technology that can be used to modify or correct precise regions of our DNA to treat serious diseases**

Dr. Emmanuelle Charpentier, one of our scientific founders, co-invented CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing. She and her collaborator, Dr. Jennifer Doudna, won a Nobel Prize for this groundbreaking work. As a gene-editing tool, CRISPR/Cas9 has revolutionized biomedical research and enabled medical breakthroughs.  
  
CRISPR/Cas9 edits genes by precisely cutting DNA and then harnessing natural DNA repair processes to modify the gene in the desired manner. The system has two components: the Cas9 enzyme and a guide RNA.

![Screen Shot 2023 10 06 at 12 28 49 PM](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/_850x469_crop_center-center_none/Screen-Shot-2023-10-06-at-12.28.49-PM.png)

**Rapidly translating a revolutionary technology with the goal of developing transformative therapies.**

Rapidly translating a revolutionary technology with the goal of developing transformative therapies.

### **CRISPR Lexicon**

A variety of genetic edits can be performed with CRISPR/Cas9, including:

### CRISPR/Cas9 Gene Editing

**DISRUPT**

![](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/DNA-Disrupt.jpg)

*CRISPR/Cas9 can make a single cut using one guide RNA; the cut is then repaired through natural processes, which can result in the addition or deletion of base pairs, leading to gene inactivation.*

**DELETE**

![](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/DNA-Delete.jpg)

*A segment of DNA can be removed by using two guide RNAs that target separate sites; after cleavage the two separate ends are joined together while the intervening sequence is removed.*

**CORRECT OR INSERT**

![](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/DNA-Correct.jpg)

*Adding a genetic template alongside the CRISPR/Cas9 machinery can enable the cell to correct a gene or insert a new gene.*

### **Therapeutic Approach**

With innovation at our core, we aim to develop potentially transformative gene-based medicines. We are applying gene editing to treat disease in two primary ways:

**Genetic diseases: Targeting specific genes that cause or influence the course of a disease**

Many diseases, both rare and common, have a genetic basis. The scientific understanding of how specific genes are involved in disease is advancing rapidly, offering the opportunity to use gene editing technologies to disrupt or correct disease-related genes.

**Cell therapies: Engineering the next generation of cell therapies that can address an array of diseases**

Cell therapies have begun to make a meaningful impact in numerous diseases, including cancers. With our technologies, we can engineer cell therapies with the aim of making them more effective, safe and widely available.

In either case, we may edit cells *ex vivo* (outside the body) or *in vivo* (inside the body).

![Ex Vivo](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/_500x577_crop_center-center_none/ExVivo_2023-10-19-222804_jawx.jpg)

![Ex Vivo](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/_500x577_crop_center-center_none/ExVivo_2023-10-19-222804_jawx.jpg)

![CRISPR Program Gaphics Mechs](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/_500x577_crop_center-center_none/CRISPR-Program-Gaphics-Mechs.jpg)

![CRISPR Program Gaphics Mechs](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/crisprtx-assets.investeddigital.com/_500x577_crop_center-center_none/CRISPR-Program-Gaphics-Mechs.jpg)

We’re also pushing the bounds of science as we continue to advance new gene editing technologies. [Learn more here](https://crisprtx.com/focus-areas/crispr-x).

Our development of transformative new investigational medicines will involve working closely with patients, families, healthcare professionals, regulatory agencies and other groups dedicated to improving healthcare. We are focused on the treatment of somatic cells, which do not pass DNA on to children. We are not using human germline modifications, which could be passed from parents to children, and we support the [current recommendations](https://www.isscr.org/guidelines) of the International Society for Stem Cell Research in this regard.

[Pipeline](https://crisprtx.com/pipeline)

URL: https://cdn.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/blobs/f351/10052255/508e8c6dd1ea/13346_2023_1320_Figa_HTML.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the application of CRISPR/Cas9 technology in gene therapy, showing various delivery systems such as viral particles, polymer, and lipid nanoparticles, combined with Cas9 and guide RNA components to facilitate gene editing.

enriched_points.expanded_content: CRISPR technology enables precise modifications of DNA, facilitating advancements in gene therapies for genetic disorders, cancers, and infectious diseases.
New gene editing techniques, including base editing and prime editing, enhance the accuracy and efficiency of genetic modifications compared to traditional methods.
Clinical trials using CRISPR have shown promising results, such as successful treatments for beta-thalassemia and sickle cell disease, highlighting its therapeutic potential.
Recent years have seen regulatory bodies granting approvals for CRISPR-based therapies, marking a significant milestone in the acceptance of gene editing in clinical settings.
CRISPR technology is reshaping treatment paradigms, offering potential cures for genetic diseases and shifting focus from symptom management to disease modification.
